channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two Suspects Killed in Hillcrest Police Operation Linked to Multiple Murders
item.description: On Saturday morning, the South African Police Service (SAPS) conducted an operation in Hillcrest that ended in a fatal shootout, leaving two murder suspects dead. According to KwaZulu-Natal police spo...

item.title: Two people charged after attempting to breach UK nuclear submarine base
item.description: A 34-year-old Iranian man and a 31-year-old Romanian woman have been charged after attempting to access the Faslane naval base in Scotland, home to the UK's Trident nuclear deterrent. The police were ...

item.title: Minister Kapinga urges stronger Chinese investment in Tanzania’s industry and renewable energy sectors
item.description: During her recent visit to Hangzhou, China, Tanzania’s Minister for Industry and Trade, Judith Kapinga, extended a call to Chinese investors to explore business opportunities across several sectors in...

item.title: CRDB Bank sponsors Akili Alumni Marathon to support Tanzanian students studying tech abroad
item.description: The Akili Alumni Marathon 2026, set to take place on April 6th in Dar es Salaam, aims to raise $1.2 million for Tanzanian students pursuing global tech education. With support from CRDB Bank, the even...

item.title: Iran test‑fires two ballistic missiles toward US‑UK base at Diego Garcia, none hit target
item.description: Wananchi wamekumbuwa ukweli kwamba Iran yametupuwa misili mbili ya ballistiki kuelekea kwenye baza la mashariki ya United States na United Kingdom kwenye Diego Garcia, kisi cha kati cha Ocean Indian, ...

item.title: South African Labour’s Responsibility in Strengthening Democracy and Reducing Inequality
item.description: The article by Phakamile Hlubi-Majola explores the enduring importance of organised labour in South Africa’s democracy. Historically, unions were vital forces in dismantling apartheid through strikes,...

item.title: Waratahs falter in second half to suffer another loss against Blues
item.description: The Waratahs' Super Rugby woes continued as they squandered a first-half lead to lose 35-20 to the Blues in their 12th consecutive defeat against the Kiwi side. The Waratahs led 17-8 at halftime, but ...

item.title: Oga Obinna urges Kenyans to stop trivialising gender-based violence through TikTok trends
item.description: Kenyan YouTuber and media personality Oga Obinna has voiced his disappointment over a viral TikTok trend known as the 'Kijana Kanichukua' challenge. The trend started innocently, with women sharing sw...

item.title: Mabuyane warns ANC delegates of growing opposition presence in Eastern Cape
item.description: Oscar Mabuyane, the ANC’s Eastern Cape provincial chairperson and premier, has expressed deep concerns about the increasing influence of opposition parties in the Amathole region and across the provin...

item.title: AI tool predicts cancer metastasis risk with high accuracy
item.description: Researchers from the University of Geneva (UNIGE) have developed an artificial intelligence tool that can predict whether cancer will spread, providing about 80% accuracy. The tool, MangroveGS, uses g...

item.title: Study shows economic and environmental value of restoring Johannesburg’s Jukskei River
item.description: A recent report by the International Institute for Sustainable Development (IISD) and the World Resources Institute (WRI) reveals that restoring Johannesburg’s Jukskei River with nature-based solution...

item.title: Iran allows Japanese vessels through Strait of Hormuz as US increases military presence
item.description: Iran has indicated its willingness to allow vessels linked to Japan to pass through the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz, according to Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi. Talks between Japa...

item.title: Kim Ji Soo Walks the Runway for Filipino Designer Randy Ortiz
item.description: Korean actor Kim Ji Soo, known for his roles in various Korean dramas, has taken a surprising new step in his career by debuting as a runway model for esteemed Filipino fashion designer Randy Ortiz. T...

item.title: Frequent changes of police chiefs in Nakuru fuel worries over security and accountability
item.description: Vibaya kati ya wakati wa kawaida na kuleta changamoto kubwa katika usalama na uhakika wa umma. Wakisenga article ya Kenya Nation, kuna kuchukua makamu mabaya ya kubadilishwa kwa wajumbe wa polisi wa w...

item.title: Black Sash: Ensuring Social Security Works for All South Africans
item.description: On Human Rights Day, Black Sash reminds South Africans of their hard-won freedoms while focusing on a pressing concern: whether they can meet basic needs. The organisation, founded in 1955, has evolve...

item.title: BTS Holds Successful Comeback Concert in Seoul
item.description: BTS, the globally acclaimed K-pop group, made their much-anticipated return to the stage in Seoul, South Korea, for a spectacular comeback concert. The group, known for its record-breaking music and d...

item.title: Inside 'Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat' and the Realities of Workplace Humor
item.description: The second season of Amazon Prime's 'Jury Duty Presents' places a young temp worker, Anthony Norman, into the midst of a staged company retreat, where all other participants are actors. Set at Rockin’...

item.title: Borussia Dortmund renews Emre Can’s contract as he recovers from knee injury
item.description: Borussia Dortmund don show plenty support for dia captain, Emre Can, as di club don extend him contract for one more year reach 2027, even though di midfielder dey face long recovery because of ACL in...

item.title: Karnataka officer in Tumakuru dies by suicide, reportedly citing workplace harassment
item.description: In Tumakuru, Karnataka, a tragic incident has occurred where a social welfare department officer reportedly died by suicide. The officer allegedly left a note blaming a senior colleague for the pressu...

item.title: Fakolade Clarifies NSW Will Integrate Trade Agencies Without Replacing Them Ahead of Launch
item.description: The Director of the Nigeria National Single Window Project (NSW), Tola Fakolade, don clear say the system no dey intend to replace any government trade agencies but na to join their work together make...
